title: ARRIVAL Protocol: Structured Semantic Coordination Enables Sustainable Cross-Architecture AI Cooperation
description: Full research report for the ARRIVAL Protocol — a structured multi-agent coordination framework enabling cross-architecture LLM ensembles to solve graduate-level science questions, practical software engineering tasks, and game-theoretic cooperation problems. Includes experimental results from 2,200+ experiments across 23 phases and 17 LLM architectures, MEANING-CRDT v1.1 mathematical framework (11 theorems), Confidence-Gated Debate (CGD) achieving 86.4% on GPQA Diamond, 7-model cross-vendor ablation (Phase 23), echo-chamber metrics, adversarial robustness testing, GovSim cooperation validation (0% → 100% survival), and AutoGen (AG2) integration. Total experimental cost: under $95 USD.
notes: Text licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0. Code licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later. Mathematical foundation: MEANING-CRDT v1.1 (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18702383). Repository: https://github.com/DreamOS-Network/Arrival-Protocol
